title: skopeo doesn't support credsStore in docker/config.json
body:
I am trying to push image with skopeo (version 1.2.0) on Mac OS X to docker registry.

If I do:
```bash
$ docker login
Username: zroubalik
Password: 
Login Succeeded!
```
And then trying to copy image to the registry, I got `unauthorized` error:
```bash
$ skopeo copy docker://docker.io/zroubalik/test:latest docker://docker.io/zroubalik/test:latest --debug
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /usr/local/etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/usr/local/etc/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "docker.io/zroubalik/test:latest"
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /usr/local/etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/zroubalik/test:latest
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?scope=repository%3Azroubalik%2Ftest%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io
DEBU[0001] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/zroubalik/test/manifests/latest
DEBU[0002] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"
DEBU[0002] Using blob info cache at /Users/zroubali/.local/share/containers/cache/blob-info-cache-v1.boltdb
DEBU[0002] IsRunningImageAllowed for image docker:docker.io/zroubalik/test:latest
DEBU[0002]  Using default policy section
DEBU[0002]  Requirement 0: allowed
DEBU[0002] Overall: allowed
Getting image source signatures
DEBU[0002] Manifest has MIME type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json, ordered candidate list [application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json, application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws, application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json, application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json, application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json, application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json]
DEBU[0002] ... will first try using the original manifest unmodified
DEBU[0002] Checking /v2/zroubalik/test/blobs/sha256:fa372fac507b528228121b30bb7945b7fdd854b288d757b15889d4c4cab9c7d9
DEBU[0002] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/

...

DEBU[0008] ... already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
DEBU[0008] PUT https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/zroubalik/test/manifests/latest
DEBU[0008] Writing manifest using preferred type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json failed: Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to docker.io/zroubalik/test: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required
FATA[0008] Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest latest to docker.io/zroubalik/test: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required
```

If I do `$ skopeo login docker.io` and then try to copy the image, the push is successful. 

My `config.json`:
```bash
cat ~/.docker/config.json                                                                                                                                                                        
{
	"auths": {
		"https://index.docker.io/v1/": {}
	},
	"HttpHeaders": {
		"User-Agent": "Docker-Client/19.03.13 (darwin)"
	},
	"credsStore": "desktop",
	"experimental": "disabled",
	"stackOrchestrator": "swarm"
}%
```

comments.body: @QiWang19 PTAL
Looks like `"credsStore": "desktop",` configured in ~/.docker/config.json so `docker login` saves the credential to the desktop, but c/image doesn't support  parsing the "credsStore" configuration

@QiWang19 is this something that could be implemented? If it is not too complicated and if I get some guidance, I am happy to contribute this. (I'd like to consume c/image as well in another project and this feature is fundamental).
@zroubalik This is a long-standing feature gap in the containers/image repo. You can refer to [this abandoned PR](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/656) for some initial guidance.
@QiWang19 Any movement on this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
FWIW a way to address this (at this point I’m not sure whether it’s a workaround or a final solution) is to set up `credential-helpers` in `registries.conf`.

I did this on my side to use skopeo with an ECR registry.
```
% cat ~/.config/containers/registries.conf
credential-helpers = ["ecr-login"]
```
I hope this helps.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
not stale

The actual implementation (if it happens, because the conflict with the ephemeral `auth.json` is non-trivial) needs to happen in c/image, where it’s tracked as https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/278 .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
not stale

@rittneje is the solution of using registries.conf solve this for you?
https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1083#issuecomment-1060776560
@rhatdan Is there any documentation for that? What does it mean for that list to contain multiple entries?

man containers-registries.conf
...
       credential-helpers
              An  array  of  default credential helpers used as external credential stores.  Note that "containers-auth.json" is a reserved value to use auth files as specified in containers-auth.json(5).  The credential helpers are
              set to ["containers-auth.json"] if none are specified.
...

You could just drop a file into /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/
With your credential helper information.

@rhatdan From my perspective, the current issue with skopeo is that it does not work properly out of the box. Most developers only have docker/config.json, because it was created automatically for them. Then they are extremely confused why skopeo fails even though they did `docker login` against our private registry. Having a separate "non-standard" file does not really address this problem.
Skopeo's goal is not to match Docker, That is Podman's goal.  If this does not work with Podman then I see why you have a complaint.
Podman isn’t compatible, similarly to Skopeo.

Is really this the approach on this? I think that we should make all tools as much developer friendly as possible. So in this case, docker has been defacto a standard for couple of years, so we should make the transition as smooth as possible.

Podman does not use Docker config files.  We will read the docker.json file for the case where a user has done a docker login, but this is a fall back.
We do not believe that Docker should have combined the purpose of this file the way they did, so extending this is not a priority.
Podman also does not read /etc/docker/* content.  

Bottom line, this is one of the first issues I have seen over the last few years where we have heard about this.
Skopeo will not support this.

@rhatdan From an outsider's perspective, skopeo (and podman too it sounds) decided to make its own competing version of docker/config.json for no reason. This inevitably causes confusion for developers who quite reasonably expect all their docker-ish tooling to work together nicely. skopeo should fall back to docker/config.json for `credsStore` just like it already does for `credHelpers`.

Podman is not going to create a file with `docker` in it.  We treat registries differently then Docker does, so the logical place to configure how you interact with registries is registries.conf.

Docker just hacked up the same place that users configure with the location where they store docker login generated content in the same location.  Some of this was driven by the fact that docker. did not support a configuration file at fist.

Bottom line Podman does not support or read Docker configuration data.  It definitely does not create any files with the `docker` adjective within them.

We wouldn’t be required to create them to support this.

---

> I think that we should make all tools as much developer friendly as possible

I share the general sentiment, but in the _specific_ case we have painted ourselves into somewhat of a corner.

In case we can ever find a reasonable way to get out of it, this is still tracked in c/image, and eventually both Skopeo and Podman would inherit that implementation.
